codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 20 findings. Key confirmed blockers: AC5 simulation approach broken when S1 implemented (finding 1); HOME-based test breaks script invocation path (finding 2); AC3 only covers --pre-head drift, not grant.head_sha mismatch as distinct failure (finding 3); warn verdict behavior under-specified in operation order (finding 5); no AC for CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset exit code 2 (finding 7); no AC covers M12/M13 commands/push.md changes (finding 9); AC1 mock capture path underspecified (finding 14). Remaining findings (4, 6, 8, 10, 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20) classified below.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Findings 1, 3, 5, 7, 9 incorporated as blocking issues. Findings 2, 14 incorporated as major issues. Findings 4, 6, 8, 10, 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 18, 20 classified as in_scope_minor or out_of_scope per BA-validation rules. Finding 17 classified out_of_scope (path injection hardening not in user requirement). Finding 19 classified out_of_scope (security wording accuracy not a requirement blocker).

issues.title: AC5 simulation approach is infeasible when S1 (step 6) is implemented
issues.description: AC5 GIVEN says: 'all conditions valid AND push.sh path does not exist or is not executable (simulated by pointing HOME at a tmpdir without push.sh)'. However, the operation order (step 6) specifies S1: 'Verify ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh exists and is executable'. If S1 is faithfully implemented, redirecting HOME to a tmpdir without push.sh causes the script to abort at step 6 — BEFORE the sentinel is written at step 11. Therefore the M11 sentinel-cleanup code path (try/except OSError after execv) is never exercised by this simulation. The AC5 check ('the sentinel file that was written before the execv attempt is removed') cannot be satisfied because no sentinel is ever written. The AC tests the wrong failure mode. The spec text says 'simulated by pointing HOME at a tmpdir' but what this actually tests is S1-abort (push.sh missing pre-consume), not M11-sentinel-cleanup (push.sh present but execv fails). These are two distinct code paths.
issues.proposed_fix: Split AC5 into two separate ACs: (a) S1 failure AC: push.sh missing or non-executable causes step-6 abort, exit 1, no sentinel written, grant not consumed — simulated by redirecting HOME so ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh does not exist; (b) M11 cleanup AC: push.sh exists and passes the step-6 executable check, but execv raises OSError at step 13 — simulated by replacing the push.sh file with a script whose shebang points to a non-existent interpreter (e.g. '#!/nonexistent/bin') so os.execv raises FileNotFoundError — assert sentinel is unlinked and exit code is 1.

issues.title: AC3 does not cover the grant.head_sha mismatch failure path as a distinct case
issues.description: The spec defines two separate HEAD-related abort paths: (1) step 5 — current HEAD does not equal --pre-head argument (early drift check before reading grant); (2) step 9 — grant.head_sha does not equal current HEAD (second drift check binding the grant). AC3 GIVEN only simulates scenario 1 ('a commit was made between Step 2 and execute-push.py invocation'). Scenario 2 is distinct: current HEAD equals --pre-head (passes step 5), but the grant was written for a different HEAD (grant.head_sha != current HEAD, fails step 9). This can occur if the push-analyst ran at a different HEAD than the one at Step 2 snapshot. AC3 as written ('HEAD does not equal --pre-head') tests only scenario 1 but the spec claims both are covered under 'HEAD drift'. A developer could implement only the step-5 check and skip step-9 entirely, passing all 5 ACs. The spec_text says AC3 covers HEAD drift but the literal GIVEN only simulates one of the two drift paths.
issues.proposed_fix: Either: (a) rename AC3 to 'pre-head mismatch' and add AC6 for the grant.head_sha mismatch case (GIVEN: current HEAD equals --pre-head, but grant.head_sha in the grant JSON contains a different SHA; expect exit 1, no sentinel, stderr contains 'head_sha' or 'grant binding mismatch'); OR (b) expand AC3 GIVEN to explicitly test both paths in sequence and require both error paths to produce the 'HEAD drift' substring in stderr.

issues.title: verdict=warn behavior is not specified in the 13-step operation order
issues.description: Edge case 4 states: 'verdict=warn: script must display risks[] to stderr as a warning and then proceed (not abort). Only verdict=blocked aborts.' But the 13-step operation order only mentions verdict=blocked at step 8 ('if verdict=blocked, display risks and exit 1'). There is no step that handles the warn case. The operation order says step 8 validates all 9 grant fields — but does not say when/where the warn risks[] display happens in the execution sequence. A developer following the operation order literally would have no explicit point to insert the warn display. Additionally, no AC tests the warn path at all. M6 explicitly requires warn to proceed, but the operation order is silent on the mechanism. This is an investigation_completeness gap: the spec identified the warn case in edge cases but did not fully specify where it fits in the ordered execution flow.
issues.proposed_fix: Add explicit sub-step at operation order step 8: 'If verdict=warn: display risks[] to stderr as WARNING (do not abort); continue to step 9'. Add a warn-path AC: GIVEN a valid grant with verdict=warn and non-empty risks[]; WHEN execute-push.py is invoked with a mock push.sh; THEN exit via mock push.sh (process replaced via execv), risks[] contents are displayed on stderr before exec, and the sentinel is written with result=PASS.

issues.title: Missing AC for CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset / exit code 2 path
issues.description: M3 specifies: 'Script aborts with exit 2 if CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset/empty'. The exit-code table documents exit 2 as 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID not set, or required CLI argument missing'. However, none of the 5 acceptance criteria (AC1-AC5) test this path. The exit 2 code is part of the public interface contract of the script. Without an AC, dev can implement this as exit 1 instead of exit 2 and all tests pass. Given this is a safety-critical script where the orchestrator must detect the error condition, the exit code differentiation is verifiable contract, not an implementation detail.
issues.proposed_fix: Add AC: GIVEN CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is unset (or empty string); WHEN execute-push.py is invoked with otherwise valid arguments; THEN exit code is exactly 2 (not 1); AND stderr contains 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID'; AND no grant file is read and no sentinel is written.

issues.title: M12/M13 (commands/push.md updates) have zero acceptance criterion coverage
issues.description: M12 requires that commands/push.md Steps 4+5 be updated: Step 4 instructs the orchestrator that execute-push.py is the sole grant validator/consumer; Step 5 replaces the bash block with the python3 invocation. M13 requires the invocation path in commands/push.md to be 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py'. These are binding Must Have requirements but no AC verifies them. AC1-AC5 only test the script's runtime behavior. A developer could ship the script without updating commands/push.md at all (or update it incorrectly) and all 5 ACs would pass. The rm -f instruction at commands/push.md:167 that BA's own spec says must be removed has no AC to enforce its removal.
issues.proposed_fix: Add two ACs: (AC-M12) WHEN grep 'rm -f.*push-analyst' commands/push.md is run, THEN exit code is 1 (no such line remains); AND grep 'execute-push.py' in Step 4 area returns a reference. (AC-M13) WHEN grep 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md is run, THEN output contains the invocation with --repo-hash, --branch, --remote, --request-id, --pre-head arguments.

issues.title: AC5 HOME-based simulation also breaks the script invocation path itself
issues.description: Separate from QA-1: if the test sets HOME to a tmpdir and then invokes 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py', the script file itself does not exist at that path. The AC specifies invocation as 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' but this path will fail with 'No such file or directory' before the script even starts, not with the M11 sentinel cleanup behavior. The test harness must either invoke by absolute path or set up the tmpdir with a symlink.
issues.proposed_fix: In any AC that uses HOME redirection for testing, specify that the harness invokes execute-push.py by absolute path (e.g. python3 /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/scripts/execute-push.py), or creates the tmpdir with the necessary structure (tmpdir/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py as a symlink). Add this clarification to the AC GIVEN.

issues.title: AC1 mock push.sh capture path is underspecified — test harness cannot read the captured data
issues.description: AC1 GIVEN specifies 'a mock push.sh at ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh records its argv[1] and CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID env to a temp file then exits 0'. The THEN clause checks 'captured from mock push.sh's recorded env'. But the spec does not define: (a) what temp file path the mock writes to, (b) how the test harness discovers that path after the Python process is replaced via os.execv, or (c) how the assertion code reads the captured file. After os.execv, the Python process is gone — the test harness runs in a separate parent process. Without a defined capture path, this AC cannot be mechanically verified. The spec says 'captured from mock push.sh's recorded env' but does not specify the capture mechanism.
issues.proposed_fix: Specify the capture mechanism explicitly: the test harness sets an environment variable (e.g. EXECUTE_PUSH_TEST_CAPTURE=/tmp/execute-push-test-capture-$$.json) before invoking execute-push.py; the mock push.sh reads this env var and writes argv[1] and environment as JSON to that path; the harness asserts the file exists and contains CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID and argv[1]=remote after execute-push.py's process terminates.

issues.title: Operation order step 9 is redundant with step 8 (grant.head_sha already validated in M4/step 8)
issues.description: M4 specifies grant validation includes 'head_sha==current git HEAD'. This is executed at step 8. Step 9 separately says 'Validate grant.head_sha == current HEAD (second drift check, binding the grant)'. If step 8 already validates head_sha, step 9 is redundant. Codex finding 4 also flagged this. This is a minor spec clarity issue — either step 8 should validate schema/types only (deferring binding checks to step 9), or step 9 should be removed and the head_sha check documented as part of step 8. As written, the spec is self-contradictory about when head_sha is validated.
issues.proposed_fix: Clarify the distinction: step 8 validates schema and structure (field types, verdict enumeration, risks is array, expires_at format); step 9 validates binding (head_sha == current HEAD, nonce == request-id, branch == BRANCH, etc.) as a separate post-schema step. Or merge them and remove step 9. Either is acceptable but must be explicit.

issues.title: Grant consumption behavior for blocked verdict is unspecified and inconsistent with current commands/push.md
issues.description: The current commands/push.md Step 4 consumes (unlinks) the grant BEFORE acting on the verdict ('Consume (unlink) the grant' at line 165-168, then 'Act on verdict' at lines 170-173). The new operation order consumes at step 10, after verdict check at step 8 — meaning a blocked grant is NOT consumed. This is a behavioral change from the current spec. The BA spec does not document this as an intentional design choice. If the grant is not consumed on blocked verdict, a second /push invocation with the same request-id could re-read the blocked grant and re-display the same risks. Whether this is desired (audit trail) or undesired (replay potential) is not stated.
issues.proposed_fix: Add explicit statement in BA spec: 'When verdict=blocked, the grant file is intentionally left unconsumed [because X] OR is consumed [because Y].' Either choice is valid but must be documented. Add test for whichever behavior is chosen.

issues.title: Step 5 replacement bash block uses non-executable placeholder syntax
issues.description: The commands/push.md Update Specification (Step 5 change) shows: '[--auto if --auto was passed to /push]' inside a bash code block. This is not valid bash syntax. A developer or orchestrator treating the code block as executable would encounter a bash parse error. The correct form would be either two separate invocation examples (with and without --auto) or a conditional bash snippet.
issues.proposed_fix: Replace '[--auto if --auto was passed to /push]' with a bash conditional comment or two concrete variant examples. Suggested: add a note '# Append --auto if the /push invocation included --auto' and show: 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py ... # without --auto' and 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py ... --auto # with --auto'.

out_of_scope_codex_findings.title: No AC verifies --auto forwarding
out_of_scope_codex_findings.rationale: M2 and M10 both specify --auto forwarding. AC1 happy path does not explicitly test --auto. This is a test coverage gap dev should address, but BA spec correctly specifies the requirement in M2/M10. Not a BA spec defect.

out_of_scope_codex_findings.title: AC1 does not prove os.execv rather than subprocess
out_of_scope_codex_findings.rationale: The user requirement explicitly states os.execv (process image replacement). M10 is explicit. A static/AST check in an AC would be ideal but is a test quality gap, not a BA spec defect. R6 acceptance criterion ('push.sh is exec'd not subprocess'd') does specify this.

out_of_scope_codex_findings.title: AC1 sentinel assertion unrealistic vs real push.sh
out_of_scope_codex_findings.rationale: Real push.sh reads and deletes the sentinel. The mock should capture sentinel contents before deletion. This is a test design detail. BA spec correctly requires sentinel to be written; dev test design should handle this.

out_of_scope_codex_findings.title: AC1 sentinel fields check should assert exact set of keys
out_of_scope_codex_findings.rationale: BA spec and Contract C both say 'do not add extra fields'. An exact key set assertion would be stronger. Test quality gap, not BA spec defect. Correct for dev to implement.

out_of_scope_codex_findings.title: 'All 9 grant fields' count is ambiguous
out_of_scope_codex_findings.rationale: M4 lists 9 fields to validate: file existence, valid JSON, nonce, branch, head_sha, remote_name, session_id, verdict, risks, expires_at — that is actually 10 check items (file existence + 9 JSON fields). Codex counts 8. The ambiguity in counting is minor. The fields themselves are listed explicitly in M4. Dev should count from M4's list, not from the '9' label.

out_of_scope_codex_findings.title: Path segment injection validation missing
out_of_scope_codex_findings.rationale: User requirement does not include path traversal hardening. request-id format validation (^[0-9a-f]{32}$) may be reasonable defensive coding but is not a stated requirement. Out of scope for this cycle.

out_of_scope_codex_findings.title: Grant consume should use os.unlink and fail closed on race
out_of_scope_codex_findings.rationale: Concurrent invocation race hardening is acknowledged in Edge Case 7 as an existing design limitation. The spec does not require atomic consume-or-fail semantics. This is adjacent hardening beyond the user requirement.

out_of_scope_codex_findings.title: Security window wording overclaims
out_of_scope_codex_findings.rationale: Accuracy of security prose is a documentation quality issue. The core requirement (use os.execv to eliminate the && timing window) is correctly specified. Wording softening is optional.

out_of_scope_codex_findings.title: No AC covers valid expires_at with trailing Z
out_of_scope_codex_findings.rationale: Edge case 5 explicitly documents the Z-suffix parsing requirement and the fix (replace Z with +00:00). Technical hints also specify this. Test coverage gap for dev, but BA spec correctly documents the edge case.

out_of_scope_codex_findings.title: blocked verdict grant consumption semantics inconsistent
Exit-code taxonomy incomplete for setup failures
out_of_scope_codex_findings.rationale: The exit-code table documents 0, 1, 2. Missing detail for all sub-cases is a documentation gap. S2, S3 (should-have) cover repo-hash and branch cross-checks with exit 1. Dev can document internally.

out_of_scope_codex_findings.title: Step 5 replacement block non-executable placeholder syntax

evidence_quality.notes: BA correctly derived sentinel schema from hooks/push.sh:118-152 (tier_2_verified). Grant schema from commands/push.md:144-168 read in session. Root cause is accurately characterized as a missing artifact (not a regression). The '9 grant fields' count is ambiguous (actually 10 check items including file-existence + JSON validity). Operation order step 9 creates an internal contradiction (validated at step 8 already). These are minor evidence quality issues, not evidence fabrication.

scope_alignment.notes: BA correctly identified that both Step 4 and Step 5 must be updated (scope expansion from user-reported 'Step 5 only' to 'Steps 4+5'). M12/M13 are explicitly in scope. The issue is that ACs don't enforce M12/M13 (QA-5), not that the scope itself is wrong.

investigation_completeness.notes: BA investigated the sentinel schema from push.sh and grant schema from push-analyst.md. The warn verdict path was identified in Edge Cases but not fully specified in the operation order (QA-3). Codex was consulted and 9 findings were incorporated. The investigation is thorough but has the warn-path specification gap.

affected_file_accuracy.notes: scripts/execute-push.py confirmed absent (bash exec). commands/push.md:144-211 confirmed to contain the Step 4/5 instructions being replaced. hooks/push.sh:84-189 confirmed as sentinel read target. File paths verified by direct Read in this session.

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.notes: Multiple drift instances: AC5 literal GIVEN ('pointing HOME at tmpdir') will not exercise M11 when S1 is implemented (QA-1). AC3 literal GIVEN ('HEAD does not equal --pre-head') does not cover grant.head_sha mismatch as a distinct failure path (QA-2). AC1 mock capture path described but not specified (QA-7).

verified_checks.evidence: push.sh line 110 confirms 60s mtime check. os.execv replaces process image so no inter-process gap exists. BA description is accurate.

M2 specifies: --repo-hash, --branch, --remote, --request-id, --pre-head, --auto. M3 specifies CLAUDE_SESSION_ID from env. Exit code table complete for primary paths. Consistent with what push.sh expects (CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID env var at push.sh:96).

verified_checks.check: Operation order: sentinel NOT written before all abort checks pass
verified_checks.evidence: 13-step operation order places sentinel write at step 11, after: args parse (1), env read (2), repo-hash check (3), branch check (4), HEAD vs pre-head (5), push.sh exists (6), grant read (7), grant validation (8), grant.head_sha vs HEAD (9), grant consume (10). All abort conditions precede sentinel write.

push.sh:137-150 validates: result, request_id, head, branch (raw), remote — all 5 fields mandatory non-empty strings. BA M9 specifies exactly: {result: PASS, request_id, head, branch (raw), remote}. The sentinel path uses branch-encoded, sentinel JSON uses raw branch — confirmed correct at push.sh:94 (BRANCH_RAW) vs line 140 (BRANCH_REF = raw branch).

push.sh lines 44-59 parse args with 'while [ $# -gt 0 ]', case '*) REMOTE=$1'. M10 specifies: ['push.sh', remote] + (['--auto'] if auto else []). argv[0]='push.sh' is conventional program name (ignored in bash args), argv[1]=remote is received as $1, --auto as $2. This is correct.

verified_checks.check: CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID must be set before execv
verified_checks.evidence: push.sh:96 reads CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID for sentinel binding check. M10 and operation order step 12 specify os.environ['CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID'] = request_id BEFORE execv. Consistent.
agents/push-analyst.md Phase 7 shows GRANT_PATH=${GRANT_DIR}/<REQUEST_ID>.json where GRANT_DIR=/tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst/<REPO_HASH>/<SESSION_ID>. BA spec specifies identical path structure.

verified_checks.check: commands/push.md Steps 4+5 update scope is correct
verified_checks.evidence: commands/push.md:165-168 currently has rm -f unlink instruction in Step 4. commands/push.md:211 has the exec pattern in Step 5. BA spec correctly identifies both must be updated (M12 covers Step 4, M12+M13 cover Step 5). Scope alignment confirmed.

verified_checks.check: expires_at Z-suffix parsing edge case documented
verified_checks.evidence: Edge case 5 explicitly documents: 'datetime.fromisoformat in Python < 3.11 does not parse trailing Z; use replace("Z", "+00:00")'. Technical hints repeat this. push-analyst.md Phase 7 uses strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ') confirming Z-suffix format. BA documentation is accurate.

qa.approval_notes: The BA spec for execute-push.py is substantively correct in its technical design: the operation order is sound (all abort conditions before sentinel write), the sentinel schema exactly matches push.sh, the exec argv structure is correct, and the timing window analysis is accurate. The spec has also incorporated the original Codex feedback constructively. HOWEVER, the spec has 5 blocking defects in its acceptance criteria and operation order specification that must be corrected before dev proceeds: (1) AC5 simulation approach will not exercise M11 sentinel-cleanup when S1 is implemented; (2) AC3 does not cover the grant.head_sha mismatch as a distinct failure path; (3) the warn verdict behavior is not specified in the operation order; (4) no AC covers the CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset/exit-2 path; (5) no AC enforces the commands/push.md update requirements (M12/M13). Two additional major issues: the AC5/AC1 HOME-based test invocation path is ambiguous, and the AC1 mock capture mechanism is underspecified. These gaps mean dev could implement a partial script that passes all 5 ACs but: skips M11 cleanup, treats warn as approved without displaying risks, uses exit 1 for missing CLAUDE_SESSION_ID, and never updates commands/push.md. BA must fix the ACs before this spec is actionable for dev.

blocking_issues: QA-3 (warn verdict unspecified in operation order)
refined_context.recommended_approach: BA should: (1) Split AC5 into two ACs covering S1-failure and M11-cleanup separately, using a broken-shebang push.sh for M11 test; (2) Add AC6 for grant.head_sha mismatch with HEAD==pre-head; (3) Add step 8b to operation order: 'if verdict=warn, display risks[] to stderr and continue'; (4) Add AC for CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset exiting code 2; (5) Add ACs for M12/M13 (commands/push.md rm -f removed, execute-push.py reference present); (6) Specify mock capture path mechanism for AC1; (7) Fix the [--auto] placeholder in the Step 5 bash block. All technical correctness of the spec is sound — these are purely test coverage and specification completeness gaps.
